feedback.text: I would like this act to ensure that video games - which now are most often purchased from digital stores such as Steam, Epic Games Store, or Itch - not be killed. Some games require a computer server that is run by the publisher to be able to be played. The publisher retains access to the software run by these servers. When a publisher stops that software from being run ever again, the game is effectively "killed". It cannot be played. In simple terms, the publisher could release a simplified version of the server software, allowing players to run their own servers at no cost to the publisher. This would allow the game to be preserved, played and studied.
